sourceTitle: Build the perfect workflow with customizable layouts

questions.question: How do you open the Layout Builder for a database page?

options.text: Click the three‑dot menu in the top‑right corner of the page
options.explanation: The three‑dot menu contains other page settings, not the layout builder.

options.text: Hover over the database page title and select “Customize layout”
options.explanation: Hovering over the title reveals the “Customize layout” option, which opens the builder.

options.text: Press Ctrl + L on the keyboard
options.explanation: There is no keyboard shortcut for opening the layout builder.

options.text: Right‑click anywhere on the page and choose “Edit layout”
options.explanation: Right‑clicking does not provide a layout‑editing option.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the purpose of the Details panel?

options.text: It displays the database’s filter and sort settings
options.explanation: Filters and sorts are managed elsewhere, not in the Details panel.

options.text: It provides a collapsible side area for less‑prominent properties
options.explanation: The Details panel is a right‑side collapsible area for “nice‑to‑have” details.

options.text: It automatically creates a new page for each database entry
options.explanation: Creating new pages is unrelated to the Details panel.

options.text: It replaces the Main page area when the page width is narrow
options.explanation: The Details panel does not replace the Main page area; it is supplemental.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of properties you can pin under the page title?

options.text: Two
options.explanation: The limit is higher than two.

options.text: Four
options.explanation: Pinned properties can hold up to four important properties.

options.text: Six
options.explanation: Six exceeds the supported limit.

options.text: Unlimited
options.explanation: The guide explicitly states a limit of four.

questions.question: When you add a property as a Module, what happens to that property in the Property group?

options.text: It remains in the Property group and also appears as a Module
options.explanation: A property used as a Module is removed from the Property group.

options.text: It is hidden from the Property group entirely
options.explanation: Modules take the property out of the Property group, showing it only in its own area.

options.text: It duplicates, showing in both places
options.explanation: Duplication does not occur; the property moves.

options.text: It becomes read‑only in the Property group
options.explanation: Read‑only status is unrelated to Modules.

questions.question: Which layout configuration would be most appropriate for a photography portfolio database?

options.text: Place a large image module in the Main page area and pin title and date at the top
options.explanation: The guide suggests using a large visual element in the Main page for visual‑focused databases like photography.

options.text: Put all metadata in the Details panel and leave the Main page empty
options.explanation: A photography portfolio benefits from visual prominence, not an empty main area.

options.text: Pin only the camera settings and hide the photos
options.explanation: Hiding photos defeats the purpose of a visual showcase.

options.text: Create a separate database for each photo and link them
options.explanation: The guide focuses on layout within a single database, not splitting into many.

questions.question: In a centralized task database, how can teams ensure they see only the properties relevant to their workflow when creating linked views?

options.text: By setting a global filter that hides all other properties
options.explanation: Filters affect rows, not which properties are displayed in a view.

options.text: By customizing the layout so team‑specific properties are in the Details panel and selecting those for the view
options.explanation: Teams can choose which properties from the layout (especially those in the Details panel) to show in their linked view.

options.text: By duplicating the database for each team
options.explanation: Duplication defeats centralization.

options.text: By disabling the Details panel for all users
options.explanation: Disabling the panel would hide useful info, not tailor views.

questions.question: What advantage does using a Property group section in the Details panel provide for cross‑functional teams?

options.text: It automatically syncs the section with external project‑management tools
options.explanation: The guide does not mention external syncing.

options.text: It lets each team add its own sub‑properties without cluttering the Main page
options.explanation: Sections in the Details panel allow teams to add specific info while keeping the main view clean.

options.text: It restricts all users to edit only the pinned properties
options.explanation: Editing permissions are not controlled by property groups.

options.text: It converts the database into a spreadsheet format
options.explanation: Layouts do not change the underlying database type.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why layouts are considered more than a visual upgrade?

options.text: They replace the need for filters and sorts by rearranging data
options.explanation: Filters and sorts remain separate functionalities.

options.text: They enable users to tailor which properties are visible and where, improving information hierarchy and efficiency
options.explanation: Layouts let you prioritize and organize properties, affecting how users interact with data.

options.text: They automatically generate summary reports based on pinned properties
options.explanation: Layouts do not generate reports.

options.text: They allow pages to be exported as PDFs with custom designs
options.explanation: Exporting is unrelated to layout customization.

questions.question: If a property is both pinned and placed in a module, what will you see on the page?

options.text: The property appears twice: once under the title and once as a module
options.explanation: A property cannot occupy both places simultaneously; adding it as a module removes it from pinned.

options.text: The property only appears as a module; it is removed from the pinned section
options.explanation: When you add a property as a module, it is taken out of the pinned area.

options.text: The property disappears entirely from the page
options.explanation: The property will still appear, but only in the module location.

options.text: Notion shows an error and prevents you from adding the property as a module
options.explanation: Notion allows the move; it simply reassigns the property.

questions.question: Which scenario most clearly demonstrates the benefit of moving the Property group to the Details panel?

options.text: A recipe database where the cook time, servings, and cuisine are pinned at the top
options.explanation: This uses pinned properties, not moving the entire group.

options.text: A product roadmap where each team’s metrics are organized into separate collapsible sections in the Details panel
options.explanation: Moving the Property group lets teams have dedicated sections without crowding the main view.

options.text: A media tracker that displays a large gallery in the Main page area
options.explanation: The gallery is a module, unrelated to the Property group location.

options.text: A simple to‑do list that only uses the default property layout
options.explanation: No need to relocate the Property group for a basic list.
